lines.text: Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is trying to move Joey's chair and not having much luck at it as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, y'know I was thinking of moving the couch over here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (laughs) Why would you want to do that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So that there will be a decent place for me to sit.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Rach, there is a decent place to...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And your lap does not count! Okay? Come on help me move this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No. No. No.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No. Rosita does not move.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry, Rosita? As in...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: As in Rosita does not move.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, it's just a chair! What's the big deal?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The big deal is that it is the exact equal distance from the bathroom to the kitchen and it's at the perfect angle so you don't get any glare coming of off Stevie.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Stevie the TV?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (glaring at her) Is there a problem?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! (Joey sets his beer and bag of chips down and heads into his room.) Oh what does he know! Come on Rosita, us chichas got to stick together! (She tries pulling on the back of the chair, until the hinge breaks and the back falls off.) You bitch!
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Chandler and Ross are on the couch talking. Phoebe is getting coffee.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, y'know what's weird? After you guys get married, when you introduce me to people you're gonna have to say, "This is my brother-in-law Ross." Not, "My friend Ross," "brother-in-law Ross." That's weird isn't it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Couldn't I just say, "This is Ross?"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (disappointed) Sure, do whatever you want.
Phoebe sits down between Chandler and Ross.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering, carrying a newspaper) Hey Ross! So, I was checking out the uh, real estate section...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look at this. (Hands him the newspaper.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, it looks like mom and dad's house. Oh, it even has a tree with a broken limb out front and the uh, the window in the attic is...Oh my God!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What? What happened to the window in the attic?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe mom and dad are selling the house!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't believe they-they didn't even tell us!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can't believe I still don't know what happened to the window in the attic!
Ross calls his parents on his cell phone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (on phone) Uh, hello dad! Monica and I just saw the house in the paper! (Listens) Yes we're surprised! (Listens) Who did you leave a message with?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (knocking on the window while outside) Sorry! (Runs off.)
Joey and Rachel's, Joey is examining the injury to Rosita while Rachel is apologizing to him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, Joey I am so sorry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I told you not to move it! Rach, how would you feel if say, I wanted to move you mom, and you said don't, and I did it anyway and her head fell off?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, come on-Joey, I'll buy you a new one! All right? We'll go down to the store right now and we'll-we'll get you a new chair.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (slowly turning and glaring at her) She's not even cold yet!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But don't you think Rosita would've wanted you to move on? I mean y'know, she did always put...your comfort first.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's true.
Rachel turns for the door and makes the "Wow!" face.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (grabbing her coat) Okay? You ready?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I... (Shuts off the TV.) I don't want Stevie to see her like this.
Monica and Chandler's, Ross and Monica are still going on about the house.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't believe we have to say goodbye to the house we grew up in. Man, some-some stranger's gonna be living in my room.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, after 15 years of mom and dad keeping it as a shrine to you, it's time the velvet ropes came down.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: They kept your room for a while.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh please! Dad turned my room into a gym 20 minutes after I moved out! I gotta say, a tanning bed and a stack of Victoria's Secret catalogues, not a gym!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, you know they love you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: As much as they love you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I was their first born! They thought she was barren! It's not my fault.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ugh, I hate this year!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's wrong with this year?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well okay, it's already February and I've only given two massages and they were both the worst tippers in the world!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That was me and Ross.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh that's right!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, y'know if you want to pick up some extra cash? Some friends of mine made good money doing telemarketing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh that's a great idea. You're really good on the phone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah and yeah, and it would probably be better than the last telephone job I had. Y'know, I probably wouldn't have to say spank as much. (Monica and Ross are shocked.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah, like you never called!
The telemarketing office, Phoebe is getting shown to her desk by the supervisor.

lines.speaker: Supervisor
lines.text: So basically this is very easy. You read from the script and try to sell as much toner as you possibly can.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I can do that! Oh, by the way, I love my office.

lines.speaker: Supervisor
lines.text: (laughs) Why don't we do a trial run.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh okay. Umm, all right. (Picks up the phone and starts reading from the script.) Hi, this is Phoebe from Empire Office Supplies, can I speak to your supply manager please?

lines.speaker: Supervisor
lines.text: I'm the supply manager.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, okay I would like to talk to you about your toner needs.

lines.speaker: Supervisor
lines.text: We don't need any toner.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh okay, well I'm sorry to bother you. Bye-bye. (Hangs up the phone.) Yeah you're right, this is easy.

lines.speaker: Supervisor
lines.text: Okay, what was wrong with that call?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh well, all right...um, no offense, but you were kind of rude.

lines.speaker: Supervisor
lines.text: They're always going to tell you they don't need toner, but that's okay because whatever they say, you can find the answer to it here in this script.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Supervisor
lines.text: So, I think you're ready to sell toner, do you have any last questions?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. (Pause) Oh wait yes! I do, I do have one question. What is toner?
Joey and Rachel's, Chandler is entering.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey! Joe! (Sees that he's not here and starts investigating. He picks up the bag of chips.) Full bag. (He picks up the beer.) Beer's still cold. Something terrible must've happened here! (He decides it's not that important; sits down on Rosita, and the back falls off causing him to flip over.) Oh no-no-no-no-no-no! (Runs over to Stevie.) Stevie, I was never here! (Runs out.)
Ross and Monica's parent's garage, Ross and Monica are arriving to go through their things. Mr. Geller is in the garage.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dad?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I'm here!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering with Monica) Hey!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Hi. God, it seems like just yesterday you guys used to come out to watch me work.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dad, we-we can't believe you're selling the house.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well, it's time for a new family to start their memories here and hopefully their check will clear before they find the crack in the foundation and the asbestos in the ceiling.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Monica) Let's grab our stuff and get the hell out of here.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I'm sorry we can't store your childhood things anymore.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, that's okay, I can't wait to see everything again! All of the memories...

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well, I don't know what's in the boxes down here, but I do know there are six or seven Easy Bake Ovens in the attic.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I used to love to play restaurant.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, not as much as you used to love to play uncooked batter eater.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, it is unreasonable to expect a child to wait for a light bulb to cook brownies! (She goes to the attic.)

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: So, I think you're boxes are over here. (They walk over to them.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! Great! (Finds a pack of cigarettes.) Wait, dad who-who's cigarettes are these?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I don't know. They-they must be your mother's, but please, please don't ask her. I'll throw these away. (He puts them in his pocket as Ross finds something of interest in one of his boxes.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Cool! Dad! My report cards! Hey, check this out dad, (reading his grades) Math, A. Science, A. History, A. Gym...(He puts it away and finds something else.) Oooh, my rock polisher!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Oh look, look there's your old makeup kit!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's a clown kit! Clown kit!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well, the white seems to be untouched. (He throws it back into the box as Mr. Geller moves a tarp and makes a discovery.) Uh-oh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Y'know how the garage floods every Spring?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How are you ever going to sell this place?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I think I accidentally used Monica's boxes to keep the water away from the Porsche.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no. Dad! Dad! What...(He goes to open one of her boxes and it rips apart.) Oh God...everything's ruined! Dad, she's gonna be crushed!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: You don't secretly smoke do you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: So it's just your mother then.
The telemarketing office, Phoebe is hard at work.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (on phone) Hi, this Phoebe from Empire Office Supplies, can I speak to your supply manager please? (Listens) Earl, thanks. (Listens) Hi Earl, this is Phoebe from Empire Office Supplies I'd like to talk to you about your toner needs. (She's reading from the script.)
[Cut to Earl's office, who is played by Jason Alexander, George from Seinfeld. They cut back and forth between Phoebe's and Earl's offices with each of their lines.]

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: I don't need any toner.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm hearing what you're saying, but at our prices everyone needs toner.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Not me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: May I ask why?

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: You wanna know why. You wanna know why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I surely do!

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Okay, I don't need any toner because I'm going to kill myself.
Phoebe desperately tries to find the scripted response to that line.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (doesn't have any luck) Umm, is-is that because you're out of toner?
Commercial Break
The telemarketing office, Phoebe is still talking to Earl.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Okay, so...no toner today. Thanks anyway, bye-bye.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no wait-wait! I can't just let you hang up! Just please talk to me.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Well...I only have one thing to do today. (He looks at his board in his office that reads, "Today's Tasks: KILL SELF.") I guess I could push it back.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Now, why do you want to kill yourself?

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: It's just that I uh, have been working for ten years now at this meaningless, dead-end job and nobody here even knows I exist!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Chandler?

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: I-I'm sorry?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No look, I-I'm sure that people know you exist!

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Oh yeah? I work in a cubicle surrounded by people. I've been talking to you for five minutes now about killing myself and no one's even looked up from their desk. Hang-hang on. (To the people standing around his cubicle.) Hey everybody! Uh, I'm gonna kill myself! (There's no response; no one even looks up.) I'll get back to ya. (To Phoebe) I got nothing. Wait. (He sets the phone down.) Uh, hey Marge! (Mimes putting a gun to his head, pulling the trigger, and splattering his brain on the wall behind him. Then points to himself. Marge watches this, then goes back to work.) (To Phoebe) Ehh, nothing. Nothing.
Joey and Rachel's, Chandler has replaced Rosita with his chair.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: My chair. Now, if anybody asks, your name is Rosita! (He runs out the door, grabs the back of Rosita, and we can hear Joey and Rachel talking as they are coming up the stairs. Neither of them have reached the landing yet.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You will like it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No I won't.
Chandler runs to check on them coming up the stairs.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You don't even know!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Because, I know what I like and what I don't like! It's not the same thing!
Chandler throws the back of Rosita into his apartment and quickly starts pushing the base into his apartment.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well look, if you don't like this...(The audience's laughter at Chandler's progress cuts out the rest of Rachel's line.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know why you say that so soon.
Joey and Rachel reach the landing just as Chandler closes the door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on Joey, I just bought you a new chair! The most expensive one in the store! Hey, y'know what I was thinking? We could name her Francette.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Francette? What is she? A couch?
They enter their apartment.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Poor thing. Cut down in her prime.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, the new chair will be here in an hour. Maybe we should actually move Rosita out of here. Y'know, start the heeling process?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I guess you're right. Maybe, maybe I'll take her down to the incinerator. It's gonna be so said, and kinda cool. (He goes to remove the back, but it doesn't come off. So he sits down in it, puts his feet up, stands up, and looks back at it.) She's heeled!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's weird.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No it's not weird, it's a miracle!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's not a miracle Joey! I'm sure there's some explanation.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh there is! If you want something enough and your heart is pure, wondrous things can happen!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, I really don't...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (interrupting her) Can you tell me how this happened?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well no.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Miracle!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, y'know what? Maybe somebody came in here and fixed it! Or something!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Someone like an...angel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's right Joey, the chair angel came in and heeled your chair. (She sits down in the chair.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (angrily) Get your non-believer ass outta my chair! (She gets up and heads for her room.)
The Geller's Garage, continued from earlier. Ross and Mr. Geller are still deciding what to do.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well, she'll understand right? It's not like I did it on purpose.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dad that won't matter to her. Look, all my stuff is safe and dry and all her is-is, is growing new stuff! See, this is exactly the kind of thing that makes her think you guys love me more than you love her.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Oh my God, does she really thinks that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, can you blame her?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well I don't know, I-I suppose we may have favored you unconsciously, you were a medical marvel! The doctor said your mother could...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dad, dad I don't want to hear about it.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, not right now. Okay look, Monica came here for some memories and damnit, we're gonna give her some! Okay, grab...grab some empty boxes. Okay? We'll-we'll take stuff from mine and whatever we can pass off as hers we'll-we'll put 'em in their.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Like uh y'know like this! This! (He picks up one of those art projects that kids make in kindergarten and first grade.) She-she could've made this!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Sure!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right? And this! (He picks up a trophy) She-she could've won this!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: (grabbing a glove) This could've been hers!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure! Ooh-ooh, what about this?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Your make-up kit? I'd feel better.
Ross angrily throws the kit into one of Monica's new boxes.

Joey and Rachel's, Joey is sitting the now heeled Rosita as Rachel is sitting in the newly arrived Francette. Francette is one of those new chairs from La-Z-Boy that has and does everything except cook and go to the bathroom for you. It's got a small refrigerator under one armrest it has phone jacks for the Internet and regular phone, and so much more.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (grabbing a beer out of the chair's fridge) I am so psyched I kept this chair for myself!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, me too. (He flips up his armrest in disgust.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, how's...how's the uh, miracle chair?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah? Wow! Y'know, that this thing has speakers in the headrest!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No. Really?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! You can hook it up to your TV and you get radio!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (quietly) My chair heels itself.
The telemarketing office, Phoebe is still trying to talk Earl out of suicide.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Earl, you're not hearing me! All I'm saying is that you're not alone all right? Everybody hates the people they work with! (One of her coworkers overhears that, and she mimes that she didn't mean him.)

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: (walking past Earl's desk) Hey guy!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait, what was that? That sounded like someone being nice to you.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: No! That's just the "Hey Guy" guy. He says that to everybody! He's the worst! I'd like to take him with me!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right so Earl, let's just forget about the people at the office, okay? There-there's gotta be someone else in your life worth sticking around for! What about-what about your family, your friends, or maybe your girlfriend?

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: (laughs) Yeah! Right!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh sorry, boyfriend!

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Oh no.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, whatever! Anything!
The "Hey Guy" Guy: Hey guy!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, he's gotta go.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Okay, I should, I should probably be getting back to my thing now. See ya. (Hangs up.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! I'm not finished yet! Don't! Don't you dare hang up on me!!!

lines.speaker: Supervisor
lines.text: (walking by and overhearing that) (to the rest of the staff) The new girl's good.
The Geller's Garage, Mr. Geller and Ross are finishing up recreating Monica's memories as Monica enters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey guys! Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, I just whipped us up some Easy Bake treats, they should be ready in about three days.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: (overacting) That's a good one! Do you hear that Ross? Three days!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (overacting as well) Yeah! Yeah! (Laughs.) Oh, this will make a great memory.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (wary) Okay. So, which boxes are mine?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, these. These are yours right here. (Pointing to the boxes they just created for her.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. (Starting to go through them) Oh! A coloring book! (Holding it up.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, oh you loved that thing. You always had it with you. You never went anywhere without-without that coloring book.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (looking through it) Really? Wow! It looks like I had some trouble staying inside the lines.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nu-uh! (Grabs it and examines it.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (holding up a glove) Oh, an old glove?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Oh, yeah you loved that glove! You took it every place you went. You never went any place without that glove.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! Look at this! (Picking up a shirt.) I can't believe I even fit into this shirt! (She holds it up and it reads: Tyrannosaurus Ross.) (She turns it around and looks at it.) Oh, this is yours. (Hands it to Ross.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I don't know how that got in there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (holding up a small cowboy hat) This isn't mine. (Sets it down and looks at the rest of the boxes.) Hey, this isn't, this isn't my stuff! Ugh, Ross! (Grabs and holds up a doll.) These are your boxes! Where are my boxes?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, your boxes are umm...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dad?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well, the garage flooded sweetie and it ruined everything in your boxes. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Just mine?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I'm afraid so.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So why-why wasn't Ross's stuff ruined? (Pause) And if you say the words medical marvel I'm going to Easy Bake your head!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well, I used your boxes to divert water away from the Porsche.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So wait, Ross's stuff is fine, but I have no memories because you wanted to keep the bottom two inches of your car away from water!!!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: There was also leaves and guk and stuff.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe this! (Storms out.)

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: (To Ross) Screw it! I'm having one. (Takes out and lights a cigarette.)
Commercial Break
Monica and Chandler's, Chandler is writing a letter by the bay window as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey Chandler!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How would you like to sit in a chair that fully reclines, has a rolling massage, and speakers in the head rest?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I'd love to but I've tried that so many times they won't even let me in the store anymore.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well what if I told you, you can do it in my apartment?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (excitedly) Are you telling me that you bought the chair that is making all other lounge systems obsolete? The chair that Sit magazine called the Chair of the Year?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I just purchased the La-Z-Boy E-cliner 3000. (Which is an actual product by the way, I'm not sure about the 3000 part.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's awesome! That's great! What made you do it?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, it's a long story, but umm I broke Joey's chair...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa! You broke Joey's chair?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I thought I broke Joey's chair! That's why I replaced it with mine!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohhhhh. That's how it got fixed!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, what did you think, that-that elves came in and fixed it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Noo! (Laughs) Angels.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm gettin' my chair back! (Heads for Joey and Rachel's.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? Wh-hey!
They enter Joey and Rachel's to find that Joey has broken Chandler's chair.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, it looks like it wasn't heeled after all! Yeah! So, I guess this chair is mine now! (Sits down in it and groans.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey you broke my chair!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Your chair?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, he thought he broke your chair so he switched the chairs!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So, there was no miracle?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No Joe, no miracle.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sarcastic) Oh no this is devastating! My faith is shaken. I'm so glad I have the new chair to get my through this difficult time in my life.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh! Nice try, but you don't get that chair anymore! All right? That is my chair now! You can sit on my lap! (Joey starts to get up.) No I take that back!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think I should get the chair!
Rachel and Joey both laugh at that suggestion.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How do you figure?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because you (Points to Joey) broke a chair and you (Points to Rachel) broke a chair! The only one around here that hasn't broke a chair, is me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No-no-no! This chair's not going anywhere.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, where's the logic in that?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: The logic is, that there are two of us and we are both strong enough to break a chair in half!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So Joey breaks my chair and I get nothing!
Joey whispers in Rachel's ear to confirm his response.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's right!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you guys? Like a gang or something?!
They confer again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! We are!
Rachel whispers in Joey's ear.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We're the Cobras!
Earl's Office, Earl has his head in his hands as Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Marge) Excuse me! Can you tell me where I can find Earl? He's the supply manager around here.

lines.speaker: Marge
lines.text: Sorry, I don't know any Earl.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: (screaming) I'm right here!!!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (goes over to his desk) Earl! I'm Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Phoebe? The lady who sells toner?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, look it, you-you can't kill yourself.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: (exhales) Look, um I really appreciate your coming down...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no I can't! I can't let you do it!

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Why?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because it was fate that made me call you today!

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: I thought it was toner.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! Think about it okay? This isn't even my regular job! Okay? And my first day on the job, you're my first call! And-and somebody else might've hung up on you, but I wouldn't do that because I know about this stuff. My mom killed herself.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm not gonna give you tips! Look don't you see that this-this...this all came together so that I could stop you from doing this.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Couldn't it just be a coincidence?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, it's fate!

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: It doesn't really seem like enough to be fate.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. Well umm, okay here's a weird thing. My mother was also a supply manager.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: I'm actually the office manager.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! So was she! And! Get this, okay? Your-your name is Earl, right? Her name was Pearl, P-Earl.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Well, was there anything else?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure! (Thinks.) Umm, where are you from?

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Philadelphia.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! So was she! Oh, I've got-I've got goose bumps. (She holds out her arm.)

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: (inspecting it) Really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, y'know I'm wearing layers and it's warm.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Yeah-yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But if-no look, okay. These jerks might not care about you, but the universe does! And that says a lot!

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: (To All) Did you hear that?! I don't need you guys to care about me! Because the universe cares! The whole universe! (Laughs as everyone ignores him.) (To Phoebe) I really wished they'd care just a little bit though.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know, I don't-I don't think it's you. This is a freaky place. (To All) Hey! Guys! (Everyone looks up.) (To Earl) Oh no, it's you.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Yeah.
The Geller's Garage, Monica is picking through her ruined childhood heirlooms with Ross.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, this terrible! Everything is destroyed! Look at this. (She picks up some kind of furry thing.) It obviously meant enough for me to save it, and I don't even know what it is! Ohh, it's still soft. (She rubs it against her cheek.) What do you think this is?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right. I think it was a mouse.
Monica screams, throws the mouse down, and rubs her hands on Ross's sweater to clean them.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: (entering) How are you honey?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How do you think I am?! You've wrecked all my childhood memories. You love Ross more than me. And I just rubbed a dead mouse on my face!
Ross gets up to let his dad sit next to Monica.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Sweetheart, we love you just as much as Ross! Now, I'm sorry about everything that happened and I'd probably never be able to make it up to you, but here's a start. (He hands her a small box.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (opening it) What's this?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: It's the key to my Porsche. Well, the key to your Porsche.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (shocked) What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (even more shocked) What?!!!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I've been thinking about getting rid of it. I was driving it the other day and saw my reflection in a store window. Your mother's right, I do look like an ass.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait, you're giving me your Porsche, you're kidding me right?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well w-w-w-w-wait, w-wait, wait, wait a minute! I mean a couple of stupid boxes get wet and she gets a Porsche?!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: (To Monica) Why don't we take it for a spin?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, what about me?! I'm a medical marvel!!
Ending Credits
Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is sitting in Joey's lap on Francette, and they're both groaning.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ahhhh....

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahhh...... (To Rachel) Eh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Hey guys!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you guys know what happened to Chandler's barca lounger?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, Joey broke it. Had to get rid of it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you kidding?! I get a Porsche and the barca lounger's gone?! This is the best day ever! (Runs out.)
End
